title: The Crash That Left Lindsey Vonn in a Race to the Olympic Start Line
title_cn: 让林赛·沃恩在奥运起跑线上争分夺秒的摔倒

paragraphs.cn: 2026年1月31日 美国东部时间上午9:00

paragraphs.en: Lindsey Vonn was already in the middle of an audacious comeback. Not only had the American alpine skier returned to competition at the age of 41 on a partially rebuilt knee, she was also winning enough elite races to set her sights on a medal at the Milan Cortina Winter Olympics.
paragraphs.cn: 林赛·沃恩已经处于一次大胆复出的过程中。这位美国高山滑雪运动员不仅在41岁时带着部分重建的膝盖重返赛场，而且还赢得了足够的精英赛事，将目标锁定在米兰-科尔蒂纳冬奥会的奖牌上。

paragraphs.en: But now, the chance of her capping her return with one final golden run through the Olympics is slimmer than ever.
paragraphs.cn: 但现在，她以一次最终的奥运金牌冲刺来结束复出的机会比以往任何时候都渺茫。

paragraphs.en: On Friday morning in Crans-Montana, Switzerland, just one week out from the Olympics, Vonn suffered the latest devastating crash of her career. She was competing in the final race before the Games in her signature downhill event and limped off favoring her left knee—not the one she’d had rebuilt.
paragraphs.cn: 周五上午，在瑞士克朗-蒙塔纳，距离奥运会仅一周之际，沃恩遭遇了职业生涯中最新一次毁灭性的摔倒。她在奥运会前的最后一场比赛中参加了她标志性的速降项目，然后一瘸一拐地离开，左膝（而不是她重建的那个膝盖）明显不适。

paragraphs.en: The injury cast sudden doubt onto Vonn’s ability to compete in Italy. It also threatened to remove a medal threat from what is expected to be a strong Team USA.
paragraphs.cn: 这次受伤突然让人们对沃恩在意大利参赛的能力产生了怀疑。这也威胁到将从预计强大的美国队中移除一名奖牌争夺者。

paragraphs.en: “This is a very difficult outcome one week before the Olympics…but if there’s one thing I know how to do, it’s a comeback,” Vonn wrote on social media. She added that she was discussing the situation with her doctors and would continue to undergo exams.
paragraphs.cn: “距离奥运会仅一周，这是一个非常困难的结果……但如果说我有什么擅长的话，那就是复出，”沃恩在社交媒体上写道。她补充说，她正在与医生讨论情况，并将继续接受检查。

paragraphs.en: Vonn’s first Olympic race was set to be the downhill on Sunday, Feb. 8, ahead of the Super Bowl.
paragraphs.cn: 沃恩的第一场奥运比赛定于2月8日星期天，在超级碗之前举行。

paragraphs.en: She already was one of the most promoted athletes of the Milan Cortina Games, the rare Winter Olympian who is familiar to casual audiences. The Minnesota native’s first Olympics was in 2002 in Salt Lake City, when she was 17 years old. In recent months, she had appeared on late-night TV and magazine covers ahead of her improbable last dance.
paragraphs.cn: 她已经是米兰-科尔蒂纳奥运会最受宣传的运动员之一，是少数为普通观众所熟知的冬季奥运选手。这位明尼苏达州本地人的首次奥运会是2002年在盐湖城，当时她17岁。近几个月来，在她不太可能实现的“最后一舞”之前，她曾出现在深夜电视节目和杂志封面上。

paragraphs.en: Vonn spent six years in retirement before undergoing knee surgery, including a titanium implant, with the aim of simply leading a less painful post-skiing life. But the repairs were so successful that she returned to training, then to competition.
paragraphs.cn: 沃恩退役六年，之后接受了膝盖手术，包括植入钛合金，目的只是为了过上不那么痛苦的滑雪后生活。但修复非常成功，她重返训练，然后重返赛场。

paragraphs.en: No one before Vonn had returned to elite alpine racing in their 40s. And no one of any age had competed on the World Cup tour on a rebuilt knee. Yet Vonn was thriving: She won two races in the downhill this season and had finished no lower than fourth in her other races.
paragraphs.cn: 在沃恩之前，没有人能在40多岁时重返精英高山滑雪赛场。也没有任何年龄的人能在重建膝盖后参加世界杯巡回赛。然而，沃恩表现出色：她本赛季赢得了两场速降比赛，其他比赛的成绩也从未低于第四名。

paragraphs.en: Even the medical community was stunned.
paragraphs.cn: 甚至医学界也感到震惊。

paragraphs.en: “I would tell you the vast majority of people who put in joints like she’s got would think that she could not get back to where she is today,” said Eric Heiden, the five-time gold-medal-winning speedskater from the 1980 Olympics who is now an orthopedic surgeon.
paragraphs.cn: “我会告诉你，绝大多数植入她那种关节的人都会认为她不可能回到今天的状态，”1980年奥运会五枚金牌得主、现任骨科医生埃里克·海登说。

paragraphs.en: Vonn, a three-time Olympic medalist, was set to be a key part of perhaps the strongest women’s Olympic alpine ski team in history. She was a favorite in the downhill, where she leads the World Cup season standings, and in the alpine combined event, where two teammates add their finishing scores for one result. Vonn also was set to race the super G.
paragraphs.cn: 沃恩是三届奥运奖牌得主，她将是历史上最强大的女子高山滑雪队之一的关键成员。她是速降项目的热门选手，在该项目中她领跑世界杯赛季积分榜；在高山全能项目中，两名队友的完赛成绩相加为一个结果，她也是热门选手。沃恩还将参加超级大回转比赛。

paragraphs.en: Her U.S. teammate Mikaela Shiffrin leads all skiers, male or female, with 108 career World Cup wins. Vonn is third all-time with 84 wins, just behind retired Swedish star Ingemar Stenmark with 86.
paragraphs.cn: 她的美国队友米凯拉·席弗林以108场世界杯职业胜利领跑所有男女滑雪运动员。沃恩以84场胜利位居历史第三，仅次于退役的瑞典明星英格玛·斯滕马克（86场）。

paragraphs.en: Crashes in the downhill, Vonn’s signature event, can be especially damaging since skiers on the long, steep course can exceed 80 miles an hour. It was suffering repeated injuries from crashes that forced Vonn into retirement in 2019 with chronic arthritis in her right knee.
paragraphs.cn: 速降是沃恩的标志性项目，在该项目中，滑雪运动员在漫长陡峭的赛道上速度可超过每小时80英里，因此摔倒可能尤其具有破坏性。正是由于反复摔倒造成的伤病，迫使沃恩在2019年退役，当时她的右膝患有慢性关节炎。

paragraphs.en: The U.S. women do have depth beyond their two stars. Breezy Johnson won the downhill at last year’s world championships, and Paula Moltzan is ranked among the top five in the overall World Cup standings this season.
paragraphs.cn: 美国女子队除了两位明星之外，还有深度。布里兹·约翰逊赢得了去年世锦赛的速降冠军，而保拉·莫尔赞本赛季在世界杯总积分榜上排名前五。

paragraphs.en: But after finishing third in a recent race, Vonn had made it clear that she was going for gold at the Olympics.
paragraphs.cn: 但在最近一场比赛中获得第三名后，沃恩明确表示她将在奥运会上争夺金牌。

paragraphs.en: “I know what I have to do,” Vonn said. “I have to risk everything, and either I win or I’m in the fence.”
paragraphs.cn: “我知道我必须做什么，”沃恩说。“我必须冒一切风险，要么赢，要么出局。”

paragraphs.en: Write to Rachel Bachman at Rachel.Bachman@wsj.com
paragraphs.cn: 请发送邮件至 Rachel Bachman (Rachel.Bachman@wsj.com)

vocabulary.meaning_en: Showing a willingness to take surprisingly bold risks.
vocabulary.example: Lindsey Vonn was already in the middle of an audacious comeback.
vocabulary.example_cn: 林赛·沃恩已经处于一次大胆复出的过程中。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A return by a well-known person, especially a performer or sports player, to the activity in which they were formerly successful.
vocabulary.example: Lindsey Vonn was already in the middle of an audacious comeback.
vocabulary.example_cn: 林赛·沃恩已经处于一次大胆复出的过程中。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Relating to high mountains; specifically, relating to skiing in which the heel of the boot is fixed to the ski.
vocabulary.example: Not only had the American alpine skier returned to competition at the age of 41 on a partially rebuilt knee, she was also winning enough elite races to set her sights on a medal at the Milan Cortina Winter Olympics.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这位美国高山滑雪运动员不仅在41岁时带着部分重建的膝盖重返赛场，而且还赢得了足够的精英赛事，将目标锁定在米兰-科尔蒂纳冬奥会的奖牌上。

vocabulary.example: On Friday morning in Crans-Montana, Switzerland, just one week out from the Olympics, Vonn suffered the latest devastating crash of her career.
vocabulary.example_cn: 周五上午，在瑞士克朗-蒙塔纳，距离奥运会仅一周之际，沃恩遭遇了职业生涯中最新一次毁灭性的摔倒。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A distinctive pattern, product, or characteristic by which someone or something can be identified.
vocabulary.example: She was competing in the final race before the Games in her signature downhill event and limped off favoring her left knee—not the one she’d had rebuilt.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她在奥运会前的最后一场比赛中参加了她标志性的速降项目，然后一瘸一拐地离开，左膝（而不是她重建的那个膝盖）明显不适。

vocabulary.meaning_en: To make something seem uncertain or unlikely.
vocabulary.example: The injury cast sudden doubt onto Vonn’s ability to compete in Italy.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这次受伤突然让人们对沃恩在意大利参赛的能力产生了怀疑。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Not likely to be true or to happen.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 不太可能发生的；出乎意料的
vocabulary.example: In recent months, she had appeared on late-night TV and magazine covers ahead of her improbable last dance.
vocabulary.example_cn: 近几个月来，在她不太可能实现的“最后一舞”之前，她曾出现在深夜电视节目和杂志封面上。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A strong, low-density, corrosion-resistant, lustrous metallic element, used in strong, light alloys.
vocabulary.example: Vonn spent six years in retirement before undergoing knee surgery, including a titanium implant, with the aim of simply leading a less painful post-skiing life.
vocabulary.example_cn: 沃恩退役六年，之后接受了膝盖手术，包括植入钛合金，目的只是为了过上不那么痛苦的滑雪后生活。

vocabulary.meaning_cn: 繁荣的；兴旺的；茁壮成长的
vocabulary.example: Yet Vonn was thriving: She won two races in the downhill this season and had finished no lower than fourth in her other races.
vocabulary.example_cn: 然而，沃恩表现出色：她本赛季赢得了两场速降比赛，其他比赛的成绩也从未低于第四名。

vocabulary.meaning_en: (of an illness) persisting for a long time or constantly recurring.
vocabulary.example: It was suffering repeated injuries from crashes that forced Vonn into retirement in 2019 with chronic arthritis in her right knee.
vocabulary.example_cn: 正是由于反复摔倒造成的伤病，迫使沃恩在2019年退役，当时她的右膝患有慢性关节炎。
